# GitHub Copilot Instructions — tmc5160-ClickerMZ

This is an embedded C project targeting **PIC32MZ1024EFH064** (MIPS32 M5150 core, 200 MHz).
Toolchain: **XC32 v5.00**, DFP **PIC32MZ-EF 1.4.168**, built via `make` using a bundled Cygwin shell from the `davidcoetzee.xc-project-importer` VS Code extension.

---

## Language & style rules

- **C99 only** — no C++, no VLAs, no `_Generic`.
- Use `stdint.h` integer types everywhere. Never bare `int` for register or hardware values.
- Use `stdbool.h` booleans — not `int` flags.
- All public API functions prefixed with their module name: `stepper_`, `STEPPER_`.
- `static` for all translation-unit-local state and helper functions.
- Module state lives in `static` arrays prefixed `s_` in the `.c` file (e.g. `s_hal[axis]`).
- No dynamic memory allocation. All state is statically allocated.
- `const` on all pointer parameters that are not written.

---

## Repository layout

```
tmc5160-ClickerMZ/          ← workspace root
├── Makefile                  ← delegates to srcs/Makefile; targets: build, rebuild, clean, lib
├── libs/
│   └── libstepper.a          ← built by `make lib` — archive of stepper.o + stepper_transport.o
├── bins/
│   └── tmc5160-ClickerMZ.hex ← flashable output
├── srcs/
│   ├── Makefile
│   ├── main.c                ← test harness (ClickerMZ-specific, SD_MODE=1)
│   ├── stepper/
│   │   ├── stepper.c         ← multi-axis motion library
│   │   └── stepper_transport.c ← stateless 5-byte SPI framing
│   └── config/default/       ← MCC-generated peripheral drivers (DO NOT hand-edit)
└── incs/
    ├── stepper/
    │   ├── stepper.h         ← public API (include this)
    │   ├── stepper_hal.h     ← Stepper_HAL_t hardware abstraction struct
    │   ├── stepper_transport.h ← TMC5160_Status_t, STEPPER_WriteReg/ReadReg
    │   └── stepper_reg.h     ← register address map & bitmask #defines (internal)
    └── config/default/       ← MCC-generated headers (DO NOT hand-edit)
```

### Files you should NOT edit
- Anything under `srcs/config/` or `incs/config/` — regenerated by MCC.
- `srcs/startup/startup.S` — MikroE bootloader-compatible startup.

---

## Hardware context

### MCU & board

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Device | PIC32MZ1024EFH064 |
| Clock | FRC → PLL → 200 MHz SYSCLK, 100 MHz PBCLK2/7 |
| Board | MikroE Clicker 2 for PIC32MZ |
| Bootloader | MikroE USB bootloader (`-nostartfiles` linker flag active) |

### TMC5160 (BTT TMC5160 PRO, MikroBUS socket 1)

| Signal | Pin | Notes |
|--------|-----|-------|
| SPI2 SCK | RG6 | Dedicated |
| SPI2 MOSI | RG8 | PPS RPG8R=6 |
| SPI2 MISO | RG7 | PPS SDI2R=1 |
| CS (GPIO) | RG9 | Idle HIGH — never use MSSEN |
| UART2 TX | RB2 | PPS RPB2R=2 (debug @ 115200) |
| UART2 RX | RB0 | PPS U2RXR=5 |
| EN | RE4 | HIGH = disabled |
| DIR | RB5 | Output |
| STEP | RB3 | Output |
| DIAG0 | RB12 | Input |
| DIAG1 | RB13 | Input |

**SPI Mode 3** (CKP=1, CKE=0). MSSEN disabled; CS driven manually for atomic 5-byte frames.  
**Rsense: 75 mΩ** (BTT PRO) → `VSENSE=0`. Standard board = 110 mΩ → `VSENSE=1`.  
**Chip version**: `IOIN[31:24]` = `0x30` confirms TMC5160.

### SD_MODE status
The BTT TMC5160 PRO purchased has a **glued heatsink** — the `SD_MODE` solder bridge is inaccessible.
**Current board is hardwired SD_MODE=1 (Step/Dir mode).**
- Ramp generator, SW_MODE hardware stops, DCStep, TZEROWAIT, position compare — **not testable** on this board.
- Testable now: init, enable/disable, StallGuard, CoolStep, freewheel, DIAG routing, sensorless home (MCU stops step pulses on stall).
- TMC5160T (solderable version) is planned for future purchase to test ramp mode.

---

## Stepper library architecture

### HAL abstraction (`stepper_hal.h`)
The library has zero MCU headers. All hardware access goes through `Stepper_HAL_t`:

```c
typedef struct {
    void  (*spi_write_read)(const uint8_t *tx, uint8_t *rx, uint8_t len);
    bool  (*spi_is_busy)(void);
    void  (*cs_assert)(void);
    void  (*cs_deassert)(void);
    void  (*en_assert)(void);    /* ENN low  = enabled  */
    void  (*en_deassert)(void);  /* ENN high = disabled */
    void  (*step_set)(void);
    void  (*step_clear)(void);
    void  (*dir_set)(void);
    void  (*dir_clear)(void);
    void  (*delay_us)(uint32_t us);
} Stepper_HAL_t;
```
Set SPI fields to `NULL` for step/dir-only drivers (DRV8825, A4988). Set step/dir fields to `NULL` for pure ramp mode.

### Transport layer (`stepper_transport.h/c`)
Stateless — takes `const Stepper_HAL_t *hal` per call. No global state.
- `STEPPER_WriteReg(hal, addr, data, status*)` — single 5-byte write frame
- `STEPPER_ReadReg(hal, addr, status*)` — two frames (request then data)
- 1 µs CS guard via `hal->delay_us(1)` after every deassert

### Multi-axis state (`stepper.c`)
All state is arrays `[STEPPER_MAX_AXES]` (default 4, override: `-DSTEPPER_MAX_AXES=N`).
Guard macros: `AXIS_OK(a)` = range + initialised; `AXIS_OK_SPI(a)` = AXIS_OK + spi_write_read != NULL.

---

## API reference

### Init

```c
bool stepper_init_axis(uint8_t axis, const TMC5160_Config_t *cfg, const Stepper_HAL_t *hal);
void stepper_enable(uint8_t axis);
void stepper_disable(uint8_t axis);
```

### `TMC5160_Config_t` fields

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `drive_mode` | `TMC5160_DriveMode_t` | `STEPPER_MODE_RAMP` or `STEPPER_MODE_STEPDIR` |
| `chopper_mode` | `TMC5160_ChopperMode_t` | `STEALTHCHOP`, `SPREADCYCLE`, `AUTO` |
| `microsteps` | `uint16_t` | 1–256 |
| `irun` / `ihold` / `iholddelay` | `uint8_t` | 0–31 / 0–31 / 0–15 |
| `steps_per_mm` | `float` | Full steps per mm |
| `fclk_hz` | `uint32_t` | 12000000 (internal osc) |
| `rsense_mohm` | `uint16_t` | 75 = BTT PRO, 110 = standard |
| `invert_dir` | `bool` | GCONF.SHAFT |
| `encoder_enable` | `bool` | ABN encoder interface |
| `freewheel` | `TMC5160_Freewheel_t` | NORMAL / FREEWHEEL / LS_SHORT / HS_SHORT |
| `dcstep_vmin` | `uint32_t` | VDCMIN (0=off); SD_MODE=0 only |
| `thigh` | `uint32_t` | THIGH (0=off); SD_MODE=0 only |
| `tzerowait` | `uint16_t` | Pause at V=0 between moves; SD_MODE=0 only |
| `diag0_otpw` | `bool` | DIAG0 = overtemp pre-warning |
| `diag1_index` | `bool` | DIAG1 = microstep index pulse |
| `diag1_onstate` | `bool` | DIAG1 = on-state active step |

### Motion (SD_MODE=0 / ramp mode only)

```c
void stepper_set_ramp(uint8_t axis, const TMC5160_RampConfig_t *ramp);
void stepper_move_to(uint8_t axis, int32_t position);
void stepper_move_relative(uint8_t axis, int32_t delta);
void stepper_run(uint8_t axis, uint32_t vmax, bool reverse);
void stepper_stop(uint8_t axis);
void stepper_set_position_zero(uint8_t axis);
void stepper_move_mm(uint8_t axis, float mm);
void stepper_run_mmps(uint8_t axis, float mm_per_sec, bool reverse);
```

### Status

```c
void stepper_poll(uint8_t axis, TMC5160_MotorStatus_t *status);
bool stepper_is_stalled(uint8_t axis);
bool stepper_pos_reached(uint8_t axis);
```

### StallGuard / CoolStep / Homing (SD_MODE=1 testable)

```c
void stepper_stallguard_config(uint8_t axis, int8_t threshold, bool filter_en);
void stepper_coolstep_config(uint8_t axis, uint8_t semin, uint8_t semax);
void stepper_set_stall_callback(uint8_t axis, TMC5160_StallCallback_t cb);
/* NULL on_complete = blocking; non-NULL = non-blocking, fires from stepper_poll() */
void stepper_home(uint8_t axis, bool reverse, uint32_t creep_vmax,
                  TMC5160_HomeCallback_t on_complete);
```

### Hardware stops / velocity bands (SD_MODE=0 only)

```c
void     stepper_sw_mode_config(uint8_t axis, const TMC5160_SwMode_t *sw);
int32_t  stepper_get_xlatch(uint8_t axis);
uint32_t stepper_get_ramp_stat(uint8_t axis);
void     stepper_set_velocity_bands(uint8_t axis, uint32_t tpwmthrs,
                                    uint32_t tcoolthrs, uint32_t thigh);
void     stepper_set_position_compare(uint8_t axis, int32_t pos);
```

### Raw register access

```c
void     stepper_write_reg(uint8_t axis, uint8_t addr, uint32_t data);
uint32_t stepper_read_reg(uint8_t axis, uint8_t addr);
```

---

## Peripheral driver API cheat sheet

```c
/* SPI2 — polling */
SPI2_WriteRead(txBuf, txLen, rxBuf, rxLen);
SPI2_IsBusy();

/* UART2 — ring buffer, interrupt-driven */
UART2_Write(buf, len);
UART2_Read(buf, len);
UART2_ReadCountGet();

/* CoreTimer */
CORETIMER_DelayUs(us);
CORETIMER_DelayMs(ms);

/* GPIO */
CS_Clear(); CS_Set();    /* RG9 — SPI chip select     */
EN_Clear(); EN_Set();    /* RE4 — driver enable (ENN) */
Step_Set(); Step_Clear();
Dir_Set();  Dir_Clear();
```

All plib functions come from `definitions.h`.

---

## TMC5160 SPI protocol rules

1. Every transaction is **5 bytes** (40 bits), MSB first.
2. **CS must be LOW for the entire 5-byte burst** — never toggle between bytes (MSSEN must stay disabled).
3. **Reads are two transactions**: first frame sends address; second frame clocks data out.
4. **Status byte** = RX[0] of every frame: reset_flag, driver_error, sg2, standstill, vel_reached, pos_reached, stop_L, stop_R.
5. **1 µs CS guard time** after every deassert before next assert.

---

## Common pitfalls

- **VSTOP must be ≥ 10** in ramp mode or the chip won't stop cleanly.
- **Don't reconfigure CHOPCONF while moving** — can cause current spikes.
- **IRUN/IHOLD scale**: `irun=31` = 100 % Imax. For BTT PRO 75 mΩ VSENSE=0, full scale ≈ 2.92 A; `irun=20` ≈ 1.9 A.
- **StallGuard is velocity-dependent** — only valid above `TCOOLTHRS`. Don't home at high speed.
- **MSSEN must stay disabled** — it will deassert CS mid-frame and corrupt every transaction.
- **Never use bare `int`** for register values — always `uint32_t`.
- **SD_MODE=1 board**: ramp-mode functions compile and write SPI registers but motion won't occur — motor is in Step/Dir mode. Do not attempt to test ramp registers on this board.

---

## Build commands

```bash
make rebuild    # full rebuild → bins/tmc5160-ClickerMZ.hex
make            # incremental build
make lib        # archive only → libs/libstepper.a
make clean      # remove objs/ and other/
```

## Using libstepper.a in another project

```makefile
CFLAGS  += -I/path/to/tmc5160-ClickerMZ/tmc5160-ClickerMZ/incs/stepper
LDFLAGS += -L/path/to/tmc5160-ClickerMZ/tmc5160-ClickerMZ/libs -lstepper
```
Consumer needs: `stepper.h`, `stepper_hal.h`, `stepper_transport.h` (for `TMC5160_Status_t`).
`stepper_reg.h` is internal — consumers don't need to include it.


This is an embedded C project targeting **PIC32MZ1024EFH064** (MIPS32 M5150 core, 200 MHz).
Toolchain: **XC32 v5.00**, DFP **PIC32MZ-EF 1.4.168**, built via `make` using a bundled Cygwin shell from the `davidcoetzee.xc-project-importer` VS Code extension.

---

## Language & style rules

- **C99 only** — no C++ features, no VLAs, no `_Generic`.
- Use `stdint.h` integer types (`uint8_t`, `uint32_t`, `int32_t`, etc.) everywhere. Never use bare `int` for register or hardware values.
- Use `stdbool.h` booleans (`bool`, `true`, `false`) — not `int` flags.
- All public API functions are prefixed with their module name: `stepper_`, `TMC5160_`.
- `static` for all translation-unit-local state and helper functions.
- State variables that belong to a module are `static` globals in the `.c` file, prefixed with `s_`.
- No dynamic memory allocation (`malloc` / `free`). All state is statically allocated.
- Function-like macros only for register field access; prefer `static inline` functions otherwise.
- `const` on all pointer parameters that are not written.

---

## Repository layout

```
tmc5160-ClickerMZ/          ← workspace root
├── Makefile                  ← root Makefile; delegates to srcs/Makefile
├── srcs/
│   ├── Makefile              ← compiled by xc32-gcc; discovers .c 6 levels deep
│   ├── main.c                ← test harness / application entry
│   ├── stepper/
│   │   ├── tmc5160_spi.c     ← 5-byte SPI burst transport
│   │   └── stepper.c         ← motion library (ramp, velocity, StallGuard, homing)
│   └── config/default/       ← MCC-generated peripheral drivers (DO NOT hand-edit)
│       └── peripheral/
│           ├── clk/          ← plib_clk.c
│           ├── coretimer/    ← plib_coretimer.c  (CORETIMER_DelayUs / _DelayMs)
│           ├── evic/         ← plib_evic.c
│           ├── gpio/         ← plib_gpio.c  (pin directions, PPS config)
│           ├── spi/spi_master/ ← plib_spi2_master.c (polling, no DMA)
│           └── uart/         ← plib_uart2.c  (ring-buffer, interrupt-driven)
└── incs/
    ├── stepper/
    │   ├── tmc5160_reg.h     ← register address map & bit-field #defines
    │   ├── tmc5160_spi.h     ← SPI transport public API
    │   └── stepper.h         ← motion library public API
    └── config/default/       ← MCC-generated headers (DO NOT hand-edit)
```

### Files you should NOT edit

- Anything under `srcs/config/` or `incs/config/` — regenerated by MCC (MPLAB Code Configurator).
- `srcs/startup/startup.S` — MikroE bootloader-compatible startup.

---

## Hardware context

### MCU & board

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Device | PIC32MZ1024EFH064 |
| Clock | FRC → PLL → 200 MHz SYSCLK, 100 MHz PBCLK2/7 |
| Board | MikroE Clicker 2 for PIC32MZ |
| Bootloader | MikroE USB bootloader (`-nostartfiles` linker flag active) |

### TMC5160 (BTT TMC5160 PRO, MikroBUS socket 1)

| Signal | Pin | Notes |
|--------|-----|-------|
| SPI2 SCK | RG6 | Dedicated peripheral pin |
| SPI2 MOSI | RG8 | PPS RPG8R=6 |
| SPI2 MISO | RG7 | PPS SDI2R=1 |
| CS (soft GPIO) | RG9 | Output, idle HIGH — never use MSSEN |
| UART2 TX | RB2 | PPS RPB2R=2 |
| UART2 RX | RB0 | PPS U2RXR=5 |
| EN | RE4 | Output, HIGH=disabled |
| DIR | RB5 | Output |
| STEP | RB3 | Output (Output Compare OC4 for STEPDIR mode) |
| DIAG0 | RB12 | Input (StallGuard / fault) |
| DIAG1 | RB13 | Input |
| REFCLKO3 | RB15 | Output — 2 kHz CPU clock-health reference, intentional |

**SPI mode: Mode 3 (CKP=1, CKE=0).** MSSEN is disabled; CS is driven manually for atomic 5-byte frames.

**Rsense: 75 mΩ** (BTT PRO — differs from the standard TMC5160 110 mΩ).  
`VSENSE=0` is correct for 75 mΩ.  If the board were standard 110 mΩ, use `VSENSE=1`.

**TMC5160 chip version** expected in `IOIN[31:24]` = `0x30`.

---

## Peripheral driver API cheat sheet

```c
/* SPI2 — polling, no DMA */
SPI2_WriteRead(txBuf, txLen, rxBuf, rxLen);
SPI2_IsBusy();                          /* poll until false after WriteRead */

/* UART2 — interrupt-driven ring buffer */
UART2_Write(buf, len);
UART2_Read(buf, len);
UART2_ReadCountGet();

/* CoreTimer — delay helpers */
CORETIMER_DelayUs(us);
CORETIMER_DelayMs(ms);

/* GPIO — active-low CS example */
RG9_Clear();   /* assert CS   */
RG9_Set();     /* deassert CS */
RE4_Clear();   /* EN = LOW → driver enabled  */
RE4_Set();     /* EN = HIGH → driver disabled */
```

All plib functions come from `definitions.h` (MCC-generated umbrella header).

---

## TMC5160 SPI protocol rules

1. Every transaction is **5 bytes** (40 bits), MSB first.
   - Byte 0: `address | 0x80` for write, `address` for read.
   - Bytes 1–4: 32-bit register value, big-endian.
2. **CS must be LOW for the entire 5-byte burst**, then HIGH.  Do not let CS toggle between bytes.
3. **Reads are two transactions**: first frame clocks the address in; second frame clocks the data out.  The return value of `TMC5160_ReadReg()` comes from the second frame.
4. **Status byte** is the first RX byte of every transaction — reflects `GSTAT` bits: reset_flag, driver_error, SG2, standstill, vel_reached, pos_reached, stop_L, stop_R.
5. After CS deassert, insert a **1 µs guard time** (`CORETIMER_DelayUs(1)`) before the next CS assert.

---

## Stepper library usage patterns

### Initialise

```c
TMC5160_Config_t cfg = {
    .drive_mode    = STEPPER_MODE_RAMP,
    .chopper_mode  = STEPPER_CHOP_AUTO,
    .microsteps    = 16U,
    .irun          = 20U,        /* 0-31 scale; ~1.9 A on BTT PRO 75 mΩ */
    .ihold         = 8U,
    .iholddelay    = 6U,
    .steps_per_mm  = 80.0f,
    .fclk_hz       = 12000000U,  /* 12 MHz internal oscillator */
    .rsense_mohm   = 75U,        /* BTT PRO */
    .invert_dir    = false,
    .encoder_enable= false,
};
stepper_init(&cfg);
```

### Ramp & move

```c
TMC5160_RampConfig_t ramp = {
    .VSTART=0, .A1=1000, .V1=10000, .AMAX=5000,
    .VMAX=50000, .DMAX=5000, .D1=1000, .VSTOP=10,
};
stepper_set_ramp(&ramp);
stepper_enable();
stepper_move_to(1000);   /* absolute microsteps */
```

### Polling

```c
TMC5160_MotorStatus_t st;
stepper_poll(&st);
/* st.pos_reached, st.stalled, st.overtemp, st.sg_result */
```

### Engineering units

```c
stepper_move_mm(25.0f);             /* move 25 mm */
stepper_run_mmps(50.0f, false);     /* 50 mm/s forward velocity mode */
```

---

## Common pitfalls to avoid

- **Never set VSTOP < 10** in ramp mode — the chip will not stop cleanly.
- **Do not reconfigure CHOPCONF while the motor is moving** — can cause current spikes.
- **IRUN/IHOLD scale**: 31 = 100 % of full-scale for the given Rsense/VSENSE combo. For BTT PRO 75 mΩ VSENSE=0, full-scale is ~2.92 A at 0.325 V reference; `irun=20` ≈ 1.9 A. Calculate before changing.
- **StallGuard is velocity-dependent**: only meaningful at speeds above `TCOOLTHRS`. Don't call `stepper_home()` at high velocity.
- **Do not hand-edit MCC-generated files** — they will be overwritten on next MCC run.
- **MSSEN must stay disabled** — enabling it will deassert CS between bytes and corrupt every SPI frame.
- **Never use bare `int` or `unsigned int`** for register values — use `uint32_t`.

---

## Build notes

- The bundled Cygwin `sh.exe` / `make.exe` lives at  
  `c:/Users/davec/.vscode/extensions/davidcoetzee.xc-project-importer-2.5.51/bin/win32/`.
- The root `Makefile` creates `/tmp` in the Cygwin root at make start to suppress a noisy warning.
- Source discovery is recursive up to 6 directory levels deep under `srcs/`.
- `USE_MIKROE_BOOTLOADER=yes` passes `-nostartfiles` to the linker.
- Generated binary: `bins/tmc5160-ClickerMZ.hex`.

---

## Verification checklist (first flash)

1. `IOIN[31:24]` == `0x30` — SPI comms working.
2. `stepper_init [OK]` — all register writes accepted (no SPI error).
3. Motor moves 1000 microsteps, UART prints `pos=1000`.
4. `stall=0  ot=0` — no spurious fault.

If `VERSION` reads `0x00` or `0xFF`, scope SCK / MOSI / CS (RG9) — check CS is going LOW, Mode 3 timing is correct.
